title: Exact Sensorial Imagination — Holdrege and the Practice of Rebuilding the World Within
author: Craig Holdrege / Goethe

body.text: Craig Holdrege gives the modern practical form of Goethean science. Go out, observe, return inwardly, rebuild what was seen, go back to the phenomenon with amore faithful eye. The imagination is bound to the world by discipline, memory, correction, and love.
After Igo out and observe, Itry to be as precise as possible in picturing what Ihave experienced. Iwant to experience more vividly what Ihave observed.

The decisive act is remembering. Ordinary memory stores alabel — plant, leaf, colour. Exact sensorial imagination recreates the phenomenon in the mind'seye. Was the leaf-edge really known? Was the colour actually seen? Was the smell entered or merely named? This is amoral training — the observer discovers where he was vague.

body.caption: The inner image rebuilt — perception becoming faithful through repeated return.

body.text: Itry to be as precise as possible in picturing what Ihave experienced. This allows me to become more and more connected to what you're observing.

This separates Goethean imagination from fantasy. Fantasy does not want correction. Exact imagination welcomes it. The inward image becomes aliving sketch. Each return to the plant sharpens it. The world teaches the imagination how to become trustworthy.
But there'ssomething else. We can'tenter into it through observation alone.
Aplant followed through time. Organs unfold, mature, decay, give way. The living being is not one of its appearances — it is the movement through them. To know it, thinking must become mobile.

body.caption: The drama of transformation — the organism known only through its movement across time.

body.text: We need to utilize our faculty of imagination to connect within ourselves what is already connected within the plant, without destroying the impression.

The most important methodological sentence. The imagination is not adding unity. It is learning to participate in unity that life already has. The plant'scontinuity is real but given all at once to the senses. The observer becomes the place where the temporal organism can appear as awhole.

Observe. Remember. Rebuild. Return. Correct. Follow the becoming. Let the inner image become as mobile and flexible as the plant itself. This is exact sensorial imagination — the world rebuilt inwardly until it teaches the eye how to see.
